title: Wizard CLI manifest
description: Shape of dist/skills/cli-manifest.json. Published alongside the manifest itself so downstream consumers (notably the wizard's prebuild) can validate the manifest's structure before snapshotting it. The wizard's CONTRIBUTING.md and context-mill's CONTRIBUTING.md cover the field semantics. NOTE: this is structural validation only. Semantic naming rules — reserved-word and internal-flag collisions on `command`/`parentCommand` — are enforced by context-mill's build (parseCliBlock), not here, so a name like `help` or `skill` is rejected at build time even though this schema would accept it. Don't rely on schema validation alone to reject reserved words.

version.description: Schema version. Bumped when the manifest shape changes in a breaking way.

buildVersion.description: Context-mill release version that produced this manifest. May be 'dev', 'bootstrap', or 'empty-fallback' depending on the source.

buildTimestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the manifest was emitted.

role.description: How this skill appears in the wizard CLI: a typed 'command', a 'skill' reachable via `wizard skill <id>`, or 'internal' (hidden).

command.description: User-typed word that registers this skill as a command. Required when role is 'command'.

parentCommand.description: Optional parent command this skill nests under.

recommended.description: When true, this leaf is pre-highlighted in the family's interactive picker — so when the user invokes the family parent with no subcommand (e.g. `wizard audit`), pressing Enter without changing the selection runs this leaf. The picker still opens (discovery + consent); the recommended leaf just makes the obvious choice one keystroke. At most one leaf per family should be marked recommended.

allOf.description: A 'command' role must carry a `command` (the user-typed word that registers it). The build fills this in from the variant id when omitted, so a command-role entry without `command` should never reach the manifest.
